title: Boo Rawl
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:BooRawl-AJ9p28.png]] |name=Boo Rawl |homeworld=[[Hazzard]]{{JournalCite|9|Slaying Dragons}} |birth=[[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The Imperial driver will be here in…less than 30 minutes. Set our coordinates for hyperspace, comrade.''"
"''Name's Boo. Now keep quiet, kid, I gotta talk to your mom's folks.|Shannon Voorson and Boo Rawl|}} '''Boo Rawl''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] independent [[Free-trader|freight hauler]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hazzard]]. A staunch believer in the proposition that individuals were free to run their lives as they chose without governmental interference, Rawl possessed one of the largest private [[Music/Legends|music]] collections by [[Musician/Legends|artists]] blacklisted by the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]]. After plying his trade throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for fifteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] in protest of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Using his own, personally modified [[BD-27 Transport]], the ''[[Long Run]]'', [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Rawl delivered heavy equipment and supplies to Alliance installations across the galaxy. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Rawl was partnered with [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Deen Voorson]] to steal a [[Colony Class 23669 power generator]] from the [[Kuat Freight Port]]. Voorson hoped to use [[Nell Voorson|family contacts]] at the freight [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] to allow them to take the [[Fusion generator/Legends|generator]] before the scheduled Imperial [[Transport/Legends|transport]] arrived. Voorson's nine-year-old cousin, [[Shannon Voorson|Shannon]], arranged for the Alliance to steal the generator, and Rawl collected the [[Cargo barge|barge]] containing the generator as planned. Leaving the freight port, the Alliance plan was discovered by the Empire when the real [[36DD|pickup barge]] arrived. Rawl piloted the ''Long Run'' while [[Space warfare/Legends|under fire]], jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and safety. ==Biography== ===Makin' his way the only way he knows how=== {{Quote|Keep your laws off my ship, my gun, my music, and my body.|The summation of Boo Rawl's philosophy|}} Boo Rawl was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hazzard]] in the [[Lahara sector/Legends|Lahara sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]. In [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], at the age of sixteen, Rawl left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to work the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|spacelanes]] as a [[Free-trader|freight hauler]]. He eventually came to [[Captain/Legends|captain]] his own [[Starship/Legends|starship]], a [[BD-27 Transport]] named the ''[[Long Run]]''. Like many other independently contracted haulers operating in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Captain Rawl modified his vessel, increasing the ''Long Run''{{'s}} [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield output]] and installing a retractable [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] in the [[barge driver]]'s front section at the expense of living and storage space. Rawl combated the boredom inherent in spending [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] by listening to his collection of anti-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[rock music]]. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Rawl joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Firmly believing in the notions of free trade and free speech, his enlistment was in protest to the Imperial presence on Hazzard and in the surrounding region. Captain Rawl used the ''Long Run'' to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] heavy machinery and supplies to Rebel bases throughout the galaxy. ===Good ol' boys=== {{Quote|You sure this'll work, Deen?''"
"''For the thousandth time Boo, yes! My aunt is the docking supervisor at this port. She wouldn't have signalled for us to come if she didn't have everything at this end arranged. I didn't live through the evacuation of Echo Base just to get blown out of the sky by my own family.''"
"''I'm not nearly as worried about your family as I am about what you've done to my sublight engines.|Boo Rawl and Deen Voorson|}} In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|evacuation]] of the Alliance's [[Echo Base/Legends|main base]] on the planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], Captain Rawl was teamed with [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Deen Voorson]] to appropriate a [[Colony Class 23669 power generator]] from the [[Kuat Freight Port]] which was being shipped to an Imperial outpost in the following two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]]. Voorson had family contacts in the freight [[Spaceport/Legends|port]] and believed he could convince his aunt, [[Nell Voorson]]—who was the senior [[docking controller]]—to arrange docking clearance for an Alliance ship to steal the [[Power fusion generator|generator]] before the official Imperial transport arrived to claim it. Voorson's aunt, however, refused to help but the technician left a [[signaller]] in her possession in case she changed her mind. Five [[Standard day/Legends|days]] before the generator's expected shipping date, Voorson received a message from the signaller telling him to collect the generator from [[loading dock 42]] at 1230 hours on the day of the generator's transfer. Voorson added an [[ST box]] to the ''Long Run''{{'s}} [[transponder]] so that the freight port would identify the ship as the Imperial barge ''[[36DD]]'', which was the identity of the ship assigned to pick up the generator at 1430 hours. Despite Rawl's nervousness at Voorson fiddling with his ship's systems, the pair took the ''Long Run'' to the [[Kuat system/Legends|Kuat system]] on the appointed day. Hailing the freight port, Rawl requested permission to enter loading dock 42 to link with the [[cargo barge]] carrying the generator. A [[Unidentified Kuat Freight Port controller|controller]] informed them that they were early, and the linking technicians would not be at dock 42 to connect the barge to the driver until 1430. Rawl requested confirmation, as the message that Voorson had received specifically said 1230. The controller checked the orders, and confirmed that while the scheduled pickup time was 1230, the technician's orders still listed the time as 1430. The technicians were on break and would return to duty within the hour. With no choice, Rawl and Voorson were forced to wait until the technicians returned. Rawl whiled away the time by listening to his [[Music/Legends|music]], including the latest release by [[Billi B and the Paradise Gang/Legends|Billi B and the Paradise Gang]]. The technicians returned to duty and linked the generator cargo barge to the ''Long Run''. Checking the barge contents before preparing to leave, Rawl and Voorson were stunned to find that his aunt's nine-year-old daughter, [[Shannon Voorson|Shannon]], had stowed away aboard the barge because she wanted to join the Rebellion. Voorson wanted to take her back to the freight port immediately, but Rawl pointed out that depressurizing the dock, unlinking the barge and explaining the entire affair to [[Kuat Freight Port Security|security]] would put them under more scrutiny than they could afford, especially with the real ''36DD'' due to arrive in less than thirty [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. Reluctantly, Voorson had to agree to take Shannon with them. [[File:LongRunEscape-AJ9p34.png|thumb|right|Rawl, Voorson and Shannon escape Kuat]] As the ''Long Run'' left the dock, Shannon revealed that she had been the one who changed the pickup time on the generator to allow the Alliance to steal it, not her mother, and Rawl admitted to being impressed although he admonished her for not remembering to change the technician's orders. As the ''Long Run'' cleared the freight port's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] range, the real barge driver ''36DD'' arrived to pick up the generator. Kuat Freight Port, now aware of the deception perpetrated by the Alliance, ordered the ''Long Run'' to return to port while the ''36DD'' moved to block their escape. A [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] was also sent to intercept the barge. Voorson's aunt contacted them over the comm, ordering them to return to the dock or be fired upon. Voorson attempted to get his aunt to call off the fighters by using the fact that Shannon was aboard the ''Long Run'', but the Imperial ships refused to listen to her. Rawl ordered Voorson to man the ''Long Run''{{'s}} turbolaser and [[Space warfare/Legends|engage]] the TIE [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] while he piloted straight for the ''36DD''. The two ships sped towards each other, and Rawl hoped that the Imperial crew were not willing to risk their ship to a collision. At the last moment, the Imperial ship dived under the ''Long Run''; the shields of the two ships brushed and collapsed, but Rawl got the ''Long Run'' into clear [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and jumped to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]]. Worrying that [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictors]] had been dispatched from either the planets [[Venir]] or [[Renegg]], Rawl, Voorson and Shannon waited to see if they would be pulled out of hyperspace. After thirty minutes in hyperspace, Rawl declared that they had escaped—having traveled beyond the range of a potential [[Interdiction field/Legends|interdiction field]]—and welcomed Shannon to the Alliance. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|What if Security decides to visit us while we're waiting?''"
"''Boo, you worry as much as my friend Voren. Security'll be on break too.''"
"''Yeah, off playing Whack-a-Bothan, or Bobbing for Calamari. I hate waiting.|Boo Rawl and Deen Voorson|}} Boo Rawl was a physically fit individual with long [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that he kept tied back, and a thick beard that was confined to his chin. An independent freight hauler, Rawl believed that people were perfectly capable of living their own lives without the interference of the Empire. A fan of anti-Imperial rock music, his collection was considered to be the largest private collection in the entire Alliance, as well as the loudest. On principle, Rawl loved any track that the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] blacklisted, and he held the hope that he would one day be able to record a track with a blacklisted group. His own composition, "[[Private Property]]," showcased both his musical talents and his well-defined sarcasm and knack of making an anti-Imperial sentiment out of any phrase. The owner and operator of the ''Long Run'', Rawl loved his ship and was very protective of it. A skilled pilot, Rawl could fly both space transports and [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], and [[Astrogation/Legends|plot courses]] through hyperspace. He was well versed in operating the systems aboard the craft, such as weapons, shields and [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], and was capable of repairing transports. His years as a freight hauler gave him knowledge of [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], and a grounding in the application of first aid. Trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], Rawl kept a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] as a personal sidearm. He was also competent in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]]. An imposing individual, Rawl was considered to be streetwise and knowledgeable about the underground music scene. He was also able to program and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]], and generally carried at least one banned music [[data cartridge]] with him. ==Behind the scenes== Boo Rawl appeared in the short story ''[[Slaying Dragons]]'', which was published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[1996]]. The story was written by [[Angela Phillips]], with illustrations by [[Mike Vilardi]]. ''Slaying Dragons'' was subsequently reprinted in [[1997]]'s ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' and later on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]. However, these reprints did not reproduce the RPG material created for the original publishing, which included much of Rawl's background in the form of a biographical capsule. Rawl was later referenced in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in its entry for his homeworld, Hazzard. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|9|Slaying Dragons|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II||Hazzard}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rawl, Boo}} [[Category:Alliance to Restore the Republic members]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]]
